flashcards.definition: Low Earth Orbit (~200–2,000 km); used by ISS, Hubble, Earth observation satellites.
Geostationary Earth Orbit (~35,786 km); satellite stays over one point; used for weather, communications.
Using a planet's gravity to accelerate or redirect a spacecraft without using fuel.
Unmanned robotic spacecraft sent to explore other celestial bodies.
Global Positioning System; ~31 satellites in MEO providing precise location data worldwide.
